title: Secure CI/CD with GitHub Actions, TypeScript, Docker, and Cloud Run
description: Developers and delivery engineers who can build TypeScript and Docker projects and need to design, secure, debug, measure, and defend production CI/CD systems. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate continuous delivery outcomes, github actions execution, workflow syntax, expressions, events, refs, forks, runners, state, concurrency, caching, artifacts, and reusable automation through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate node 24, the typescript 7.0.2 native cli, typescript 6.0.2 api compatibility tooling, npm reproducibility, layered tests, quality gates, matrices, failure semantics, diagnostics, observability, and delivery improvement through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate workflow permissions, secrets, injection defense, action dependencies, full-sha pinning, codeql for actions, oidc, slsa 1.2, sbom, provenance, attestations, and verified promotion through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate docker buildx ci, npm trusted publishing, google workload identity federation, cloud run revisions, progressive traffic, rollback, incident response, and governed production transfer through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
sharedRequirements: Every activity retrieves earlier Git, quality-gate, TypeScript, Docker, accessibility, security, testing, and evidence habits before adding a delivery-system boundary.
Browser work is deterministic inspection only. Live GitHub, runner, Docker, registry, OIDC, cloud, deployment, traffic, and failure behavior is always a named authorized transfer gate.
Passing work requires exact event, revision, workflow, runner, permissions, immutable artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, rollback, and residual-limit evidence appropriate to the competency.

competencies.statement: Distinguish continuous integration, delivery, deployment, release, and feature exposure by their automation, decision, and stakeholder boundaries.
misconceptions: Continuous delivery means every successful commit must immediately receive production traffic.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish continuous integration, delivery, deployment, release, and feature exposure by their automation, decision, and stakeholder boundaries.

competencies.statement: Relate batch size, queue time, feedback latency, failure isolation, recovery cost, and learning speed without treating deployment frequency as the objective.
misconceptions: More pipeline stages always reduce delivery risk.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Relate batch size, queue time, feedback latency, failure isolation, recovery cost, and learning speed without treating deployment frequency as the objective.

competencies.statement: Trace requirement, source revision, review, checks, build inputs, immutable artifact, deployment, observation, and recovery evidence as one delivery chain.

competencies.statement: Interpret deployment frequency, lead time, change failure rate, recovery time, reliability, and contextual tradeoffs without gaming one metric.
misconceptions: High deployment frequency alone proves delivery excellence.

misconceptions: SLSA Build Level 3 means all dependencies are secure and the software is vulnerability-free.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Map actual build and source controls to SLSA 1.2 tracks and levels without claiming transitive dependency or code-quality guarantees.

competencies.statement: Use named environments for approvals, branch and tag policies, scoped secrets, wait rules, URL evidence, and deployment history.
misconceptions: An environment name alone creates an approval gate and isolates every secret.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use named environments for approvals, branch and tag policies, scoped secrets, wait rules, URL evidence, and deployment history.

competencies.statement: Request id-token write only in the deployment job and validate issuer, audience, subject, workflow ref, repository identity, event, and environment claims.
misconceptions: id-token write grants direct write access to cloud resources.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Request id-token write only in the deployment job and validate issuer, audience, subject, workflow ref, repository identity, event, and environment claims.

competencies.statement: Exchange GitHub OIDC identity through a constrained Google Workload Identity Provider and service account without storing a key.
misconceptions: Workload Identity Federation is safe with an unrestricted repository-name subject condition.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Exchange GitHub OIDC identity through a constrained Google Workload Identity Provider and service account without storing a key.

competencies.statement: Separate build, registry, deploy, runtime, traffic, and rollback identities and grant resource-scoped roles for exact operations.
misconceptions: Cloud Run Admin alone is the complete and least-privilege deployment permission set.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate build, registry, deploy, runtime, traffic, and rollback identities and grant resource-scoped roles for exact operations.

competencies.statement: Bound token lifetime, exported credentials, audience, session, audit correlation, cleanup, trust-policy changes, and incident revocation.
misconceptions: Short-lived credentials do not require monitoring or an incident response plan.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bound token lifetime, exported credentials, audience, session, audit correlation, cleanup, trust-policy changes, and incident revocation.

competencies.statement: Deploy one verified image digest with explicit service, region, configuration, runtime identity, revision suffix, labels, and no-traffic option.
misconceptions: Deploying the same image tag guarantees the same Cloud Run revision contents.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Deploy one verified image digest with explicit service, region, configuration, runtime identity, revision suffix, labels, and no-traffic option.

competencies.statement: Validate revision readiness, configuration, identity, logs, authenticated smoke behavior, changed inputs, and dependency failure before traffic.
misconceptions: Cloud Run accepting a deployment proves the application is ready for production traffic.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Validate revision readiness, configuration, identity, logs, authenticated smoke behavior, changed inputs, and dependency failure before traffic.

competencies.statement: Move bounded traffic by revision or tag, define observation window, compare user and system signals, and stop on explicit abort conditions.
misconceptions: A one-percent canary receives a representative one percent of every user and workload type.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Move bounded traffic by revision or tag, define observation window, compare user and system signals, and stop on explicit abort conditions.

competencies.statement: Restore traffic to a known healthy revision without rebuilding, verify recovery, preserve failed evidence, and coordinate incompatible data changes.
misconceptions: Redeploying old source is the fastest and most faithful rollback.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Restore traffic to a known healthy revision without rebuilding, verify recovery, preserve failed evidence, and coordinate incompatible data changes.

competencies.statement: Keep application deployment separate from public access and IAM mutation, with explicit ownership, review, audit, and recovery for each.
misconceptions: The deployment workflow should automatically make a service public for convenience.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Keep application deployment separate from public access and IAM mutation, with explicit ownership, review, audit, and recovery for each.

competencies.statement: Derive version, tag, changelog, release notes, source commit, and authorization from a protected reviewed release process.
misconceptions: A signed tag proves the tagged source passed current required checks.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Derive version, tag, changelog, release notes, source commit, and authorization from a protected reviewed release process.

competencies.statement: Build once from reviewed source, retain immutable artifacts and attestations, approve promotion, and avoid environment-specific rebuilds.
misconceptions: Rebuilding for each environment improves reproducibility because configuration differs.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build once from reviewed source, retain immutable artifacts and attestations, approve promotion, and avoid environment-specific rebuilds.

competencies.statement: Publish with npm trusted publishing on supported hosted runners using exact repository, workflow, environment, id-token permission, and npm 11.5.1 or later.
misconceptions: OIDC trusted publishing also authenticates private dependency installation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Publish with npm trusted publishing on supported hosted runners using exact repository, workflow, environment, id-token permission, and npm 11.5.1 or later.

competencies.statement: Verify package contents, pack list, version, repository identity, source commit, workflow, transparency entry, signatures, and provenance before adoption.
misconceptions: npm provenance guarantees a published package contains no malicious behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Verify package contents, pack list, version, repository identity, source commit, workflow, transparency entry, signatures, and provenance before adoption.

competencies.statement: Separate author, reviewer, builder, approver, publisher, rollback authority, emergency bypass, and audit evidence according to risk.
misconceptions: More manual approval steps always improve release safety.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate author, reviewer, builder, approver, publisher, rollback authority, emergency bypass, and audit evidence according to risk.

competencies.statement: Instrument queue, duration, critical path, cache, failure class, artifact, deployment, and recovery with bounded cardinality and privacy.
misconceptions: Recording every branch, commit, actor, test, and error as metric labels gives the best observability.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Instrument queue, duration, critical path, cache, failure class, artifact, deployment, and recovery with bounded cardinality and privacy.

competencies.statement: Set log, artifact, cache, attestation, test-report, and deployment-evidence retention from recovery, audit, privacy, quota, and cost needs.
misconceptions: Longer retention is always safer because more evidence is available.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Set log, artifact, cache, attestation, test-report, and deployment-evidence retention from recovery, audit, privacy, quota, and cost needs.

competencies.statement: Reconstruct event, workflow version, runner, inputs, dependency graph, first causal failure, retries, side effects, and downstream state before rerunning.
misconceptions: The last red step in the UI is necessarily the root cause.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Reconstruct event, workflow version, runner, inputs, dependency graph, first causal failure, retries, side effects, and downstream state before rerunning.

competencies.statement: Contain credentials and mutations, stop unsafe workflows, preserve redacted evidence, restore a known artifact, notify owners, and retain regression controls.
misconceptions: Cancelling the active workflow fully contains a delivery incident.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Contain credentials and mutations, stop unsafe workflows, preserve redacted evidence, restore a known artifact, notify owners, and retain regression controls.

competencies.statement: Govern required workflows, allowed actions, runner groups, permissions, attestations, exceptions, expiry, drills, metrics, and risk-based improvement.
misconceptions: A centrally mandated pipeline template can replace local ownership and learning.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Govern required workflows, allowed actions, runner groups, permissions, attestations, exceptions, expiry, drills, metrics, and risk-based improvement.

credential.title: Secure CI/CD with GitHub Actions, TypeScript, Docker, and Cloud Run Certificate
